compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can validate a ticket acceptance criteria block via ticket check-ac <id>
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add `check-ac` subcommand to the ticket CLI dispatcher that wraps check-acceptance-criteria.sh's AC block validation.

**Why**: The ticket system should validate its own acceptance criteria blocks without callers invoking a separate script.

**Scope**:
- IN: New `check-ac` case in `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket` dispatcher routing to check-acceptance-criteria.sh logic; integration test using a synthetic fixture exercising the subcommand through the dispatcher asserting exit codes and output match check-acceptance-criteria.sh
- OUT: Migrating callers (Epic e1b2-2653); removing check-acceptance-criteria.sh; changing AC validation logic

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `ticket check-ac <id>` is callable via the dispatcher and produces the same exit codes and output as check-acceptance-criteria.sh
  <- Satisfies: "check-ac (acceptance criteria block validation -- replaces check-acceptance-criteria.sh)"
- When this story is complete, the integration test exercises check-ac through the CLI dispatcher against a synthetic fixture and asserts output equivalence with check-acceptance-criteria.sh
  <- Satisfies: "Each new subcommand has an integration test asserting output equivalence"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] This story depends on S3 (e5cb-d369) landing first to avoid parallel dispatcher edits

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
